key_insight: CANN-EUCLID discovers sparse, interpretable hyperelastic constitutive laws directly from full-field displacement and force data, without requiring local stress measurements.
engineering_relevance: This enables constitutive model identification from a single heterogeneous test, which is especially valuable for soft biological tissues where repeated homogeneous testing is limited.
method: Combines constitutive artificial neural networks (CANNs) with the stress-unsupervised EUCLID framework.
Minimizes equilibrium imbalance from full-field data while using sparsity regularization to select compact active law terms.
Recovers or approximates isotropic and anisotropic ground-truth laws from displacement fields and reaction forces.
evidence_signal: Across benchmark cases, the method recovered representable laws with near-exact accuracy and produced forward FE simulations that matched the ground truth.
watch_item: Performance depends strongly on whether the sampled deformation states cover key nonlinear regimes; missing stiffening behavior can lead to large extrapolation errors.
paper_title: CANN-EUCLID: unsupervised constitutive artificial neural network model discovery from full-field data
